ref: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
text: Ɔniliɛ gi aabitɛ isu ya ɔnɔ ʋidi i, Aya aaple ni ye lɛ ɔniliɛ gi aaple ni isu ya ɛ, Aya aamu ni ye. Yesu ebu kusɔliɔ baka ba gi benu aŋu mɛ beboebitɛ ba lipɔ ɔ
translated: The Lord gave a sign to his people. He spoke to them, saying, "The Lord has spoken to his people." He added to them. Jesus came into Galilee preaching the gospel and healing the people there.

ref: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.”
text: Pɔ̀ Yesu egbe liboe aki ba si, “Mlaki tɔ banuvɔ ba baba mɔ klɔ; mlakítsi kude aki ba, lɛ ese linu si Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa ɛ, ba elu kanɛ kenu u.”
translated: Jesus said, "Blessed are the meek, for they will inherit the earth; blessed are those who come to me, for they are the children of Aya and Kaʋaŋaa, and they will inherit the eternal kingdom."

ref: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
text: “Bɛpani fɛ si, ‘Ɔnɔ kakaa gi aayurɔ yedze e, ɔŋwlimi kadzɛgbeplekpa aki yɛ.’
translated: Bepani said, "He cannot take your life there; his spirit will not be completely destroyed anymore."

ref: After Jesus heard about John, he crossed Lake Galilee to go to some place where he could be alone. But the crowds found out and followed him on foot from the towns.
text: Agi Yesu enu Yohanes liboe le e, asɛ ni klɔ ní katapokpa mɛ, lɛ atrɛ ní ɔkutɔ gi ebu lidze dzaa yɛ tete e. Banima benu si asɛ lɛ bɛsɛ ní ba ima nɛ mɛ ɛ, bɛga ikli lɛ lɛ bɛzɛtsani yɛ.
translated: Jesus and John came to a certain place, and he saw the people who were there. Then he said to them, "You are the salt of the earth."

ref: The disciples left and did what Jesus had told them to do.
text: Nite si badɔkpasi tiaʋa ba bɛzɛ bitɛ kiliɛ gi Yesu asi ba ɛ:
translated: I am the sinner who gave my sins to Jesus to carry them.
